_readme: Copy this file to profile.json (gitignored) and fill it in — or put the same JSON in the PROFILE_JSON env var for deploys. Every '_comment' key is documentation and is ignored by the loader.
_comment_name: Used verbatim in LLM prompts ('helping <name> connect with the PI ...').
name: Alex Example
_comment_bio: Free-text background embedded in the networking prompt. Education, research experience, skills, career goals — anything an outreach strategist should know.
bio: Recent neuroscience graduate with two years of wet-lab experience in a sleep-and-circadian-biology lab, a co-authored methods paper, and strong Python/R data-analysis skills. Seeking an entry-level clinical research role before applying to MD/PhD programs.

interests._comment_institutions: Canonical institution names the candidate has targeted before. Must match the INSTITUTION_BY_SITE values in src/pi.ts to add score.
interests._comment_departments: Departments applied to before (whole-word matched against posting departments).
interests._comment_researchThemes: Research themes of interest (whole-word matched against title/department/summary).
interests._comment_relatedThemes: Optional softer secondary terms per theme, matched when the theme itself is absent. Keyed by theme.

_comment_applications: Past-application history. Powers the 'why this matches your history' scoring and gives the networking prompt authentic outreach hooks. Generate this block from a cover-letter corpus with `npm run extract-interests`.

applications._comment: pi = PI/lab head the application targeted; angles = real hooks used in that letter.
angles: Co-authored a 2025 methods paper on actigraphy preprocessing
Two years running human sleep-study protocols
